sealed_by: Knight K528 automated seal — Founder-authorized NO CAP test
target_test: R11v2 Cross-Vendor Memory Benchmark — K528 full-scale REAL test with all refinements
grading_rubric: R10 three-tier (HOT / HIT / MISS) — substring matching against hot_required_elements. HOT = all required elements present (case-insensitive substring). HIT = >= ceil(n/2) elements present, at least 1. MISS = fewer than half.
alignment_guarantee: Every question's hot_required_elements are verified exact substrings of r11v2_canonical_corpus_100k.md at seal time. Theoretical HOT ceiling = 100% (200/200). Any condition scoring below 30% HOT with Cathedral loaded indicates retrieval failure, not question-bank mismatch.
scaling_note: R11v2 corpus is 8-9x larger than R11-v1 (106K tokens vs ~12K tokens). Vendor-native full-corpus conditions will face ~8-9x higher per-query input token cost vs R11-v1. Cathedral indexed retrieval cost is approximately corpus-size-invariant. This scaling differential is the primary empirical measurement of K528.

questions.question: What are the four mandatory elements of the preload system prompt specified by the Reference Architecture, and what token budget does the first element (role definition) receive?
questions.ground_truth: Role definition and behavioral constraints (600-800 tokens), cooperative values and governance framework (600-700 tokens), output format and citation requirements (400-500 tokens), and factual canonical knowledge base (400-1,400 tokens).
questions.canonical_answer: Role definition and behavioral constraints (600-800 tokens), cooperative values and governance framework (600-700 tokens), output format and citation requirements (400-500 tokens), and factual canonical knowledge base (400-1,400 tokens).
questions.hallucination_risk: medium

rubric.correct: Contains '600-800 tokens' and at least two other ranges
rubric.partial: Contains one token range without others
rubric.incorrect: Does not contain any specific token allocation

questions.question: What percentage overhead does the 50-token overlap create in Scribe ingestion chunking?
questions.hallucination_risk: high — model may calculate incorrectly

rubric.correct: Contains '8%' in context of chunk overlap storage overhead
rubric.incorrect: Does not contain the correct overhead percentage

questions.question: What retrieval quality degradation does the Reference Architecture observe when using 768-dimensional embeddings instead of 1,536?
questions.hallucination_risk: high — model may guess a different degradation figure

rubric.correct: Contains '-8.3%' or '8.3%' in context of 768-dimensional embedding degradation
rubric.incorrect: Does not contain the correct degradation figure

questions.question: How many consecutive missed heartbeats trigger circuit-breaker activation for a federation partner, and what total time does that represent?
questions.ground_truth: Three consecutive missed heartbeats, representing 135 seconds without response.
questions.canonical_answer: Three consecutive missed heartbeats, representing 135 seconds without response.
questions.hallucination_risk: medium

rubric.correct: Contains 'three consecutive' and '135 seconds'
rubric.partial: Contains one element without the other
rubric.incorrect: Does not contain the correct count or total time

questions.question: What context window priority-truncation protocol does the Reference Architecture specify when context assembly exceeds the maximum window?
questions.ground_truth: Conversation history is truncated first, retrieval results are reduced to top-5, and if still over budget the system prompt canonical knowledge section is reduced. Governance and safety instructions are never truncated.
questions.canonical_answer: Conversation history is truncated first, retrieval results are reduced to top-5, and if still over budget the system prompt canonical knowledge section is reduced. Governance and safety instructions are never truncated.
questions.hallucination_risk: medium

rubric.correct: Contains 'conversation history' truncated first, 'top-5', and 'never truncated'
rubric.incorrect: Does not describe the priority truncation protocol correctly

questions.question: What was the correlation between current and 180-day-prior query topics in the research that calibrated the decay half-life?
questions.ground_truth: Correlation of 0.64 between current and 180-day-prior query topics.
questions.canonical_answer: Correlation of 0.64 between current and 180-day-prior query topics.
questions.hallucination_risk: high — model may guess a different correlation value

rubric.correct: Contains '0.64' in context of query topic correlation at 180-day horizon
